candidate_summary: Clubhouse audio app early-2021 viral growth (free install) then rapid retention decline.
observed_outcome_summary: Grew from ~3.5M downloads (2021-02-01) to ~8.1M by 2021-02-15, with >10M weekly active users by ~March 2021, then the user base declined ~21% over three weeks. A hype-spike-then-decline case: a large install signal overstated durable demand.
raw_outcome_evidence: Wikipedia 'Clubhouse (app)', verbatim: 'On February 1, 2021, Clubhouse had an estimated 3.5 million downloads on a global level which grew rapidly to 8.1 million downloads by February 15.' and 'the number of weekly active users was greater than 10 million, but the user base declined 21% during three weeks ...' (Operator-fetched 2026-05-31.)

action_signal_candidates.notes: ~8.1M downloads by 2021-02-15 (from 3.5M on Feb 1). Tier-1 install action, but FREE app — weak buyer proxy. Figures are third-party app-analytics estimates.

geographic_demographic_notes: Invite-only, pandemic-era; amplified by celebrity appearances (Musk, Zuckerberg).
reviewer_notes: Recommended evidence_tier=1 (download) but FREE install => weak buyer signal; consider Tier-2 framing. HIGH-VALUE 'curiosity vs durable demand' case. Recommended designation: TRAINING. Download counts are estimates.
uncertainty_flags: self_selected_denominator: backers/users are self-selected; the receptive/uncertain/skeptical proportions of the FULL addressable market are NOT externally observable
buyer_numerator_only: the action signal gives the buyer/action-positive count, not a four-bucket distribution
free_install_not_purchase: downloads are free — over-count curiosity, not buyer commitment
estimated_counts: download figures are third-party analytics estimates that vary by source
retention_collapse: weekly actives declined ~21% within weeks of the peak
